findings.rule: Shared types imported by both FE and BE
findings.message: The backend defines its own Priority model and DTO fields rather than importing the contracts from libs/shared-types, so the spec's 'import them in both FE service and BE DTOs/controller' is only half met. This is consistent with the existing self-contained tasks module (tasks/task.model.ts, no path alias in backend/tsconfig.json) and the contract shapes are aligned, so it is not a blocker — but the FE and BE contracts can now drift independently.

The spec calls for Testing Library component tests covering the CRUD acceptance criteria, but the three *.component.spec.ts files are pure-logic specs (validate*/removePriorityById/applyPriorityUpdate) that do not render the components, submit forms, or exercise the signal/HttpClient wiring. The author documents this is forced by the sandboxed network (cannot npm install @testing-library/angular / @angular/router|forms) and matches the repo's framework-free skeleton pattern (shared/sort.spec.ts). Component logic and all backend ACs are verified, so this is recorded as a gap to close once the workspace is wired, not a block.

The supertest e2e (real 201/400/200/404 HTTP semantics) is not executed by `npm test` (jest rootDir is src and supertest is not installed). The added priorities.controller.spec.ts compensates by driving inputs through a real ValidationPipe so 400 (AC2/AC7) and 404 (AC5) are still asserted in-process. Wire supertest + a jest-e2e config in CI to run the e2e.

app.component renders <app-task-list /> and <router-outlet /> unconditionally, so the priorities views always appear beneath the task list rather than replacing it. Cosmetic; does not affect any acceptance criterion.
